activities_projects.title: Analyzing the Poet's Tone
activities_projects.objective: Identify the mood and tone of the poem
steps: Read the poem 'Try Again' aloud twice.
List three adjectives that describe the poet's tone (e.g., encouraging, firm, patient).
Find two lines from the poem that support each adjective you chose.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how word choice creates tone in poetry.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of tone with textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Deciphering the 'Task' Metaphor
activities_projects.objective: Interpret the metaphorical meaning of 'task' in the poem
steps: In the poem, the poet mentions a 'task' that should be kept in view.
Write a 50-word paragraph explaining what this 'task' represents in real life.
Discuss with a partner: Is the task the same for everyone?
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn to interpret metaphors and abstract concepts.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of interpretation and clarity of expression (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Meaning of 'Disgrace'
activities_projects.objective: Explore the poet's perspective on failure
steps: The poet says, 'If we strive, ’tis no disgrace.' Explain what this means in your own words.
Write a short response arguing why failing is not shameful as long as effort is present.
Give one real-life example of a famous person who failed before succeeding.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking regarding social stigmas of failure.
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning and relevant examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Contextual Usage
activities_projects.objective: Master formal vocabulary from the poem
steps: Look up the meanings of: 'Prevail', 'Strive', 'Heed', and 'Patience'.
Write a separate sentence for each word, but the sentence must be about a school-related situation.
Example: 'To excel in math, one must strive to solve difficult problems daily.'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply poetic vocabulary to practical, everyday contexts.
activities_projects.evaluation: Grammatical accuracy and contextual relevance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Paraphrasing Stanzas
steps: Choose the second stanza of the poem.
Rewrite the entire stanza in modern, simple prose (plain English).
Ensure you don't lose the original meaning while changing the 'old-fashioned' phrasing.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve their ability to translate poetic language into prose.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the paraphrase and clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Writing: Advice to a Younger Self
activities_projects.objective: Practice first-person narrative using poetic themes
steps: Imagine you are 10 years older. Write a short letter (80 words) to your current self.
Incorporate the phrase 'Try Again' at least twice in the letter.
Mention a specific goal you currently have and why you shouldn't give up on it.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice narrative voice and thematic application.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, tone, and use of thematic elements (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Grammar: Conditional Sentences
steps: The poem uses many 'If' sentences (e.g., 'If at first you don't succeed...').
Find three conditional sentences in the poem.
Create three new conditional sentences of your own that offer advice to a classmate.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize and construct conditional (Type 0 and Type 1) sentences.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct grammatical structure of 'If' clauses (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Persistence' Interview
steps: Interview a teacher or a family member about a time they had to 'Try Again'.
Ask three specific questions: What was the challenge? How many times did they try? What did they learn?
Write a brief summary of their story in your notebook.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop communication skills and learn from external experiences.
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of questions and summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Structure Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the repetition and its effect
steps: Highlight every time the phrase 'Try Again' appears in the poem.
Explain in 2-3 sentences why you think the poet repeats this phrase so often.
How would the poem feel different if it was only said once at the end?
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the rhetorical effect of repetition in literature.
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of insight into poetic techniques (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Theme-based Mind Map
steps: Create a mind map with 'Success' at the center.
Branch out into the qualities mentioned in the poem (Patience, Courage, Effort).
For each branch, add a quote from the poem that matches the quality.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students synthesize and categorize thematic information visually.
activities_projects.evaluation: Organization, accuracy of quotes, and visual clarity (10 marks)
